county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Taylor County formed 1858 from Bexar, Travis. County seat is Abilene. North Texas frontier region with ranching and Indian defense heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Abilene. West-central Texas county organized 1878. Abilene Public Library genealogy collection. Grace Museum. Hardin-Simmons University library. Abilene Christian University library. Check Bexar and Travis for pre-organization records. Buffalo Gap Historic Village.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Taylor County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Abilene holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Taylor County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1858 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Taylor County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1858. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Taylor County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1858. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Taylor County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Taylor County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Taylor County Area (Comanche and Kiowa)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the Taylor County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Taylor County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Taylor County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Travis County formed 1840 from Bastrop. County seat is Austin. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Austin. Central Texas — state capital county. TSLAC (Texas State Library and Archives Commission) in Austin is the central state repository. Austin History Center at Austin Public Library. Dolph Briscoe Center for American History at UT-Austin. Texas GLO in Austin. LBJ Presidential Library. Check Bastrop for pre-1840 records. Exceptional concentration of archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Travis County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1840; County Clerk in Austin holds deed books
